mnemonics: GLM FAM: Family (distribution), Link (function), And Mean (structure) are the three core components.
keyPoints: Generalized Linear Models extend linear regression by allowing response variables to have error distributions other than normal.
The model connects the linear predictor to the mean of the response variable via a link function.
Common GLMs include logistic regression (binomial family, logit link) and Poisson regression (Poisson family, log link).
commonMistakes: Using the identity link function with a non-Gaussian family, which can lead to invalid predictions (e.g., negative counts for Poisson).
Forgetting that the coefficients are on the scale of the linear predictor, not the response, and must be transformed via the inverse link for interpretation.
